title: Scribbles: Inverted curve (horizontal)
restrictions: USER-PICKED. LeWitt's late Scribbles series — continuous hand scribbling that builds tone into a form. Built with the new `scribble` generator: irregular random-walk looping marks placed by rejection sampling against a tonal field so a soft tone emerges where scribbling is dense. Form = an INVERTED CURVE (horizontal arch, ∩) — the scribble concentrates along an arch that peaks up in the middle, with open negative space above and below. Applies the R20/R21 recalibration: OPEN, loose, hand-made wandering marks with directional/gestural character and negative space — NOT dense mechanical fill, NOT legible geometry, NOT `sheets`. Monochrome; density capped (paper-rip). The 16 span arch height, band width, mark count (sparse->dense), mark size & loopiness, and hand energy.
